message:
Improve type safety in CLI authentication and API client

This commit addresses type safety issues found during security audit:

1. Login Command - Validate tier parameter instead of blind cast
   File: packages/cli/src/commands/login.ts (lines 179-181)

   Problem: The tier query parameter was cast directly to "free" | "premium"
   without validation: `tier as "free" | "premium"`. If the API returned
   an unexpected tier value (e.g., "trial", "enterprise"), the type system
   would be violated, potentially causing downstream errors.

   Fix: Validate the tier value explicitly:
   const rawTier = url.searchParams.get("tier");
   const tier: "free" | "premium" = rawTier === "premium" ? "premium" : "free";

   This ensures only known tier values are used, defaulting unknown tiers
   to "free" as a safe fallback.

2. API Client - Use type guards for error message extraction
   File: packages/cli/src/lib/api-client.ts (lines 97-101)

   Problem: Error fields were extracted with unsafe casts:
   (errorData?.error as string) || (errorData?.message as string)

   If the API returned an error object instead of string (e.g.,
   { error: { code: 500, details: [...] } }), the cast would succeed
   but produce "[object Object]" as the error message.

   Fix: Use proper type guards:
   typeof errorField === "string" ? errorField : null

   This ensures only actual string values are used as error messages,
   falling back to response.statusText or "Request failed" otherwise.

These changes improve runtime type safety without changing the API
contract or expected behavior for well-formed responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
